new_terms.context: Nvidia's GPU architecture preceding Rubin, mentioned as a baseline for performance comparison.
Nvidia's specialized hardware/software feature for accelerating Transformer model workloads.
A hardware feature in previous Nvidia architectures that doubles throughput by zeroing out specific weights.
A new feature in Rubin's Transformer Engine to boost inference performance via dynamic sparsity.
Nvidia's high-speed chip-to-chip interconnect for linking CPUs and GPUs or chiplets.
High-speed serializer/deserializer interfaces used for NVLink and other high-bandwidth connections.
A semiconductor die that reaches the maximum size limit of a lithography reticle.
Streaming Multiprocessor; the fundamental building block of Nvidia GPUs.

new_terms.suggested_translation: 最高性能模式 / 能效优化模式
new_terms.context: Nvidia power profiles for Rubin GPUs (Max-P for performance, Max-Q for efficiency)
Mechanical component in the Rubin package for heat distribution and support
Added to the Rubin package structure to provide mechanical support and avoid warpage
Thermal Interface Material between the heat spreader lid and the cold plate
Refers to a die that takes up the maximum printable area of a lithography reticle
A new memory module form factor (Solder-down Compression Attached Memory Module)
Scalable Hierarchical Aggregation and Reduction Protocol for in-network compute acceleration
Nvidia networking product featuring Co-Packaged Optics
Refers to the number of ports or connectivity of a switch die
Component on the package that converts electrical signals to optical links
Physical housing for pluggable OSFP optical modules
Architecture for expanding clusters by adding more nodes
